title: Currency Strength Meter
subtitle: Multi-Pair Signed % Change Aggregation for 8 Major Currencies Across 3 Timeframes
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: EXTRACT — currency strength formula, sign-inversion for quote currency, 3-TF lookback calibration, strong/weak thresholds. Useful as pre-filter: only trade signals where base currency is strong AND quote is weak.

core_concept.name: Currency Strength via Signed % Change
core_concept.description: For each of 8 major currencies, find all pairs where that currency appears. Compute % close change over lookback bars for each pair. If the currency is the BASE (first in pair), use the sign as-is. If it's the QUOTE (second), invert the sign. Average all signed values = currency strength score.
core_concept.formula: pct_change = (close[-1] - close[-lookback]) / close[-lookback] * 100; if currency == quote: pct_change *= -1; strength[currency] = mean(pct_changes_across_relevant_pairs)
core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: Pre-filter gate: BUY signal on EURUSD only valid when EUR is strong AND USD is weak. Adds directional confluence beyond our zone+structure gates. 3-TF lookback (M15/H1/H4) gives short/medium/long-term strength context.

strength_formula.step_1: For each currency C in [USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CHF, CAD, AUD, NZD]:
strength_formula.step_2: Find pairs = [p for p in all_pairs if C in p] (4 pairs per currency)
strength_formula.step_3: For each pair p in pairs: pct = (close[-1] - close[-lookback]) / close[-lookback] * 100
strength_formula.step_4: If C is quote currency in p: pct *= -1
strength_formula.step_5: strength[C] = mean(pct for p in pairs)

keep: Signed % change formula with quote-currency sign inversion
3-TF lookback: M15(96 bars)=24h, H1(48)=48h, H4(30)=120h — short/medium/long context
Thresholds: > +0.3% = strong, < -0.3% = weak — simple but calibratable
Pre-filter logic: base strong + quote weak = valid signal direction
discard: Fixed 8-currency set (expand to include synthetic pair components if needed)
improve: Use returns-based calculation (consistent with Part 22): pct_change → percentage return normalized
Add momentum: is currency getting stronger (strength increasing) vs already strong (peaked)?
Map strength to position size bonus: very strong base + very weak quote → full size; borderline → reduced

creviaderiv_gate.description: Currency strength pre-filter for forex pairs
creviaderiv_gate.logic: if signal.direction == 'BUY': require strength[base] > +0.1% AND strength[quote] < -0.1%; if signal.direction == 'SELL': require strength[base] < -0.1% AND strength[quote] > +0.1%
creviaderiv_gate.note: Thresholds reduced from ±0.3% to ±0.1% for entry gate (more permissive than strong/weak classification)
